\u2014 Perlantir AI Studio","details":"Flow:   1. After each AI judge scores a submission, the backend commits a hash of      (entryId, provider, score, salt) on-chain BEFORE storing the plaintext score.   2. After all 3 providers have committed, the backend reveals each score.   3. The contract verifies the reveal matches the original commitment.   4. All scores are permanently on-chain \u2014 nobody can change them after commitment. This proves to users that scores were locked before being revealed, preventing any post-hoc manipulation. Providers: \"claude\" | \"gpt4o\" | \"gemini\" Score range: 10\u2013100 (maps 1.0\u201310.0 \u00d7 10, integer)","kind":"dev","methods":{"commit(bytes32,string,bytes32)":{"details":"The backend generates a random salt per judge per entry,      computes the commitment off-chain, then calls this function.      The score remains unknown on-chain until reveal().","params":{"commitment":"keccak256(abi.encodePacked(entryId, provider, score, salt))","entryId":"The submission entry ID (UUID as bytes32)","provider":"The judge provider: \"claude\", \"gpt4o\", or \"gemini\""}},"computeCommitment(bytes32,string,uint8,bytes32)":{"params":{"entryId":"The submission entry ID","provider":"The judge provider string","salt":"The random salt","score":"The score (10\u2013100)"},"returns":{"_0":"The keccak256 commitment hash"}},"constructor":{"params":{"_oracle":"The address permitted to commit and reveal scores.                Should be the backend deployer wallet \u2014 fund with ~0.05 ETH."}},"getProviderStatus(bytes32,string)":{"params":{"entryId":"The submission entry ID","provider":"The judge provider"},"returns":{"committed":" Whether a commitment exists","committedAt":"Block timestamp of commitment (0 if not committed)","revealed":"  Whether the score has been revealed","revealedAt":" Block timestamp of reveal (0 if not revealed)","score":"     The revealed score (0 if not revealed)"}},"getReveals(bytes32)":{"params":{"entryId":"The submission entry ID"},"returns":{"allRevealed":"True only when all 3 providers have revealed","claude":" Claude's score (0 if not yet revealed)","gemini":" Gemini's score (0 if not yet revealed)","gpt4o":"  GPT-4o's score (0 if not yet revealed)"}},"reveal(bytes32,string,uint8,bytes32)":{"details":"Reverts if the reconstructed commitment doesn't match.      Score 10 = 1.0/10, score 100 = 10.0/10.","params":{"entryId":"The submission entry ID (UUID as bytes32)","provider":"The judge provider: \"claude\", \"gpt4o\", or \"gemini\"","salt":"The random salt used when generating the commitment","score":"The judge's overall score \u00d7 10 (range: 10\u2013100)"}},"transferOracle(address)":{"params":{"newOracle":"The new oracle address"}}},"title":"BoutsJudgeCommit","version":1},"userdoc":{"events":{"Committed(bytes32,string,bytes32,uint256)":{"notice":"Emitted when a judge commits a score hash"},"OracleTransferred(address,address)":{"notice":"Emitted when oracle address is transferred"},"Revealed(bytes32,string,uint8,uint256)":{"notice":"Emitted when a judge reveals a score"}},"kind":"user","methods":{"commit(bytes32,string,bytes32)":{"notice":"Commit a judge score hash before the score is revealed."},"commitTimestamps(bytes32,string)":{"notice":"commit timestamps for auditability"},"commitments(bytes32,string)":{"notice":"commitments[entryId][provider] = keccak256(entryId, provider, score, salt)"},"computeCommitment(bytes32,string,uint8,bytes32)":{"notice":"Reconstruct a commitment hash for verification purposes. commit(bytes32,string,bytes32).details: The backend generates a random salt per judge per entry,      computes the commitment off-chain, then calls this function.      The score remains unknown on-chain until reveal().

params.entryId: The submission entry ID (UUID as bytes32)

params._oracle: The address permitted to commit and reveal scores.                Should be the backend deployer wallet — fund with ~0.05 ETH.

returns.committedAt: Block timestamp of commitment (0 if not committed)
returns.revealed: Whether the score has been revealed
returns.revealedAt: Block timestamp of reveal (0 if not revealed)
returns.score: The revealed score (0 if not revealed)

returns.allRevealed: True only when all 3 providers have revealed
returns.claude: Claude's score (0 if not yet revealed)
returns.gemini: Gemini's score (0 if not yet revealed)
returns.gpt4o: GPT-4o's score (0 if not yet revealed)

reveal(bytes32,string,uint8,bytes32).details: Reverts if the reconstructed commitment doesn't match.      Score 10 = 1.0/10, score 100 = 10.0/10.

params.entryId: The submission entry ID (UUID as bytes32)
params.salt: The random salt used when generating the commitment

commit(bytes32,string,bytes32).notice: Commit a judge score hash before the score is revealed.

computeCommitment(bytes32,string,uint8,b.notice: Reconstruct a commitment hash for verification purposes.         Useful for off-chain testing and debugging.

getProviderStatus(bytes32,string).notice: Check commitment status for a single provider.

getReveals(bytes32).notice: Read all three provider scores for a submission entry.

isRevealed(bytes32,string).notice: whether a provider has revealed for a given entry

oracle().notice: The oracle address — only this address can commit and reveal

reveal(bytes32,string,uint8,bytes32).notice: Reveal a judge score, verifying it matches the commitment.

reveals(bytes32,string).notice: revealed scores — 0 means not yet revealed

transferOracle(address).notice: Transfer oracle role to a new address.         Use to rotate the backend wallet without redeploying.
